design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate themed moon levels by jumping, punching, and using gadgets to locate and destroy communication beacons and enemy infrastructure; utilize magno-boots to traverse walls and ceilings in novel spatial puzzle sequences
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management of gadgets and power-ups; navigation complexity from inverted gravity sections; exploration pressure to find hidden secret exits and routes to access full ending
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Secret agent James Pond stopping Dr. Maybe's moon-based cheese mining operation; dynamic mobility through magnetic boots and varied gadget arsenal
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Explicit Super Mario World derivation with structured hub progression; opinionated magno-boot mechanic forces upside-down navigation as core level design pillar; requires secret level discovery as progression gate—players cannot reach full ending without finding hidden routes; playable character switching (Pond vs. Finnius) with distinct mechanical identities

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Secret character unlock (Finnius) provides alternative mobility strategy; gadget discovery and sequencing allows flexible approach to obstacle navigation; exploration-gated progression softens difficulty gates but demands thoroughness

progression.upgrade_system: Gadget pickup system: Spring Shoes, Fruit Gun, Dynamite, Bomb, X-ray Specs, Jetpack, Television, Fruit Suit—each grants temporary or persistent mobility/combat advantage; character unlock (Finnius alternative) unlocks distinct traversal mechanics
progression.unlock_mechanism: Communication beacons serve as level completion gates; four colored teacups conditionally activate certain beacons; secret exits unlock hidden levels; Stiltonium extractors and Cheese Mines must be destroyed in specific levels to achieve full ending; Finnius rescued in specific level enables character swap
replayability_hooks: Hidden secret exits mirror Super Mario World design, gating full ending behind exploration completionism
Character mechanic switching between Pond and Finnius provides distinct playstyle (Finnius belly-bounce vs. Pond gadget reliance)
Gadget-locked sections encourage multiple playthroughs to optimize routing
Themed level progression (Cheese, Custard, Ice Cream sections) provides natural pacing breaks

world_building_techniques: Thematic level grouping by dairy products (cheese, custard, ice cream sections)
Punny level names ('Garden of Edam' for Eden reference) provide wordplay world-building
Moon setting with mining infrastructure establishes Dr. Maybe's operation visually
Enemy rats as hired workforce reinforce narrative setup
Character introduction of Finnius Frog as sidekick establishes companion dynamic

boss_design.philosophy: Section-culminating encounters tied to Dr. Maybe's operations; thematic alignment with dairy-product themed zones

faction_indicators: Dr. Maybe's rat workforce represents enemy faction occupying moon bases
James Pond + Finnius Frog alliance as heroic duo against Dr. Maybe's ambitions
danger_signals: Cheese mining operation as economic threat ('conquer global markets')
Stiltonium superweapon extraction as existential threat requiring destruction
Dr. Maybe's escalation from previous defeat (RoboCod) suggests recurring villain threat
environmental_storytelling: Moon cheese mines as contested resource zones
Themed infrastructure (cheese mines, Stiltonium extractors) visualizes Dr. Maybe's operation scale
Gadget distribution across levels suggests resistance assets or found technology
Secret routes and hidden levels imply covert pathways through occupied territory
